---
name: zigmodu-api
description: Add REST API endpoints to a ZigModu module. Use when adding new routes, custom handlers, middleware, or extending an existing module's HTTP surface.
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob
---

# Add REST API Endpoints

## ⛔ HARD BLOCK

**Never edit generated `api.zig`** (marked `//! ⛔ @generated by zmodu — DO NOT EDIT`). Custom endpoints go in `ext/api.zig`.

## Quick Start — Custom Endpoint in ext/api.zig

```zig
// ext/api.zig — survives zmodu regeneration
const std = @import("std");
const http = @import("zigmodu").http;
const ext_svc = @import("service.zig");  // same ext/ dir

pub const ThingApiExt = struct {
    ext: *ext_svc.ThingServiceExt,

    pub fn init(ext: *ext_svc.ThingServiceExt) ThingApiExt {
        return .{ .ext = ext };
    }

    pub fn registerRoutes(self: *ThingApiExt, group: *http.RouteGroup) !void {
        try group.get("/things/custom", customHandler, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));
    }

    fn customHandler(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
        const s: *ThingApiExt = @ptrCast(@alignCast(ctx.user_data orelse unreachable));
        const result = try s.ext.svc.doSomething();
        try ctx.jsonStruct(200, result);
    }
};
```

## Handler Patterns

### GET with path param
```zig
// Route: /things/{id}  ← curly braces, NOT :id
try group.get("/things/{id}", getById, @ptrCast(@alignCast(self)));

fn getById(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
    const s = resolve(ctx);
    const id = try ctx.paramInt(i64, "id");
    if (try s.service.getThing(id)) |entity| {
        try ctx.jsonStruct(200, entity);
    } else { try ctx.json(404, "{\"error\":\"not found\"}"); }
}
```

### GET with query params (list/paginate)
```zig
fn list(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
    const s = resolve(ctx);
    const page = ctx.queryInt(usize, "page", 0);
    const size = ctx.queryInt(usize, "size", 10);
    const result = try s.service.listThings(page, size);
    try ctx.jsonStruct(200, result);
}
```

### POST with JSON body
```zig
fn create(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
    const s = resolve(ctx);
    const entity = ctx.bindJson(model.Thing) catch {
        try ctx.json(400, "{\"error\":\"invalid body\"}");
        return;
    };
    s.service.validateThing(entity) catch {
        try ctx.json(400, "{\"err\":\"validation failed\"}");
        return;
    };
    const created = try s.service.createThing(entity);
    try ctx.jsonStruct(201, created);
}
```

### PUT with path param + body
```zig
fn update(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
    const s = resolve(ctx);
    const entity = ctx.bindJson(model.Thing) catch {
        try ctx.json(400, "{\"error\":\"invalid body\"}");
        return;
    };
    s.service.validateThing(entity) catch {
        try ctx.json(400, "{\"err\":\"validation failed\"}");
        return;
    };
    try s.service.updateThing(entity);
    try http.RenderExt.success(ctx, "ok");
}
```

### DELETE with path param
```zig
fn delete(ctx: *http.Context) !void {
    const s = resolve(ctx);
    const id = try ctx.paramInt(i64, "id");
    try s.service.deleteThing(id);
    try ctx.json(204, "");
}
```

## Available Route Methods

```zig
group.get(path, handler, user_data)
group.post(path, handler, user_data)
group.put(path, handler, user_data)
group.delete(path, handler, user_data)
group.patch(path, handler, user_data)
```

## Context Methods Reference

```zig
// Path params (use {key} in route, NOT :key)
ctx.paramInt(T, "key")           // parse path param as integer
ctx.paramStr("key")              // get path param as string

// Query params
ctx.queryInt(T, "key", default)  // parse query param with default
ctx.queryStr("key", default)     // get query string with default

// Body
ctx.bindJson(T)                  // parse body into type T (catch → 400)
ctx.body                         // raw body bytes

// Response
ctx.json(status, string)         // raw JSON string
ctx.jsonStruct(status, value)    // serialize any Zig type to JSON
ctx.text(status, text)           // plain text

// Headers
ctx.setHeader("key", "value")    // set response header
ctx.headers.get("key")           // read request header

// Render
http.RenderExt.success(ctx, msg) // {"success":true,"data":"msg"}
```

## Route Path Conventions

```
GET    /things              → list
GET    /things/{id}         → get by id     ← curly braces {id}, NOT :id
POST   /things              → create
PUT    /things/{id}         → update
DELETE /things/{id}         → delete
```

Use lowercase plural nouns. Path parameters use `{name}` syntax (curly braces).

## Error Response Pattern

```zig
// bindJson failure → 400
const entity = ctx.bindJson(model.Thing) catch {
    try ctx.json(400, "{\"error\":\"invalid body\"}");
    return;
};

// Validation failure → 400
s.service.validateThing(entity) catch {
    try ctx.json(400, "{\"err\":\"validation failed\"}");
    return;
};

// Not found → 404
if (try s.service.getThing(id)) |entity| {
    try ctx.jsonStruct(200, entity);
} else { try ctx.json(404, "{\"error\":\"not found\"}"); }
```
